message: Jake
Tonny
Well, well, if it isn't the famous person around town, the supposed serial murderer.
San, please stop with the bad jokes.
As of now, aren't you the prime suspect?
That's a misunderstanding.
Who knows?
So, what do you want from me today?
Hey, if it isn't the prime suspect in the serial murder case.
San, please stop with the bad jokes.
A bad joke, you say? With Ginny dead and Guy dead,
soon there may be no one left but you.
San
What could you possibly want? I don't think killing me would benefit you.
Is there something else?
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
Move
System
The surroundings
About Ginny
About Guy
About Kristy
About the serial murder case
About

Exit the game.
Considering the local housing situation, it's very spacious. It's a gorgeous room surrounded by windows on three sides.
It reflects the owner's taste perfectly, with not even a single cheap gold-plated product in sight. Everything is unified with quite expensive furnishings.
A grand piano, a fur rug, and a soft leather sofa are lined up.
I can't help but find it amusing that I’m now being suspected. After all, I've been the one who was suspicious until now, so I can't say much.
He looks fresh, as if all the stress that had been weighing heavily on his shoulders has completely vanished.
Maybe it's because of his job as a director, but he always has a stern expression. The wrinkles etched on his forehead might be a sign of his suffering, or perhaps,
should I say you had bad luck, or should I say you brought it upon yourself?
Brought it upon myself?
I was careless too. I never imagined you had connections with female reporters, Cindy,
and Ginny.
It’s hard to believe there was someone with such deep ties.
But you were suspected.
However, don’t you think it's a strange story?
What do you mean?
The woman who was cut up and killed in New York is related to this Fabar case in some way, right?
That's why I ended up being suspected.
You must have had some sort of connection with everyone involved.
Was the police fun? Let me know how it went next time.
So Ginny really is dead, huh? Was the delay in discovering her because she was washed away in the river?
Well,
the police said it seemed she was thrown into the river quite some time after her death.
So, you're saying she was just left somewhere after she died?
I can't really say about that.
I haven't been able to extract any detailed information.
That's true. If you're not the culprit, then it's something we can't know.
From the moment I was shown her ring, I was already convinced she wasn't alive.
Why is that?
That ring was something precious to her. Oddly enough, she dreamed of having Director Fabar's wealth.
So, you're saying Ginny loved the director?
No, that's not right. What she loved was herself. She wasn't the type of woman who could love anyone else.
I wonder why I ended up liking a woman like that.
By the way, do you know where Guy went on his trip?
Why would you think I know? I'm not close with him.
That may be true, but there are urgent reasons I want to see him.
I'm sorry, but I don't know anything.
I can't understand why you want to see Guy, but it's a pity I can't help.
I can't believe he could die too.
I've had doubts that Guy might be the murderer.
I'm the same. At least with Ginny's case, I suspected it might be Guy or maybe Kristy.
Why did you suspect Guy?
Kristy came to see Ginny, and then she disappeared saying she was going to meet Guy. It's only natural to be suspicious, right?
That's certainly true.
On top of that, Eva wrote articles about the gossip between Guy and Kristy, and Cindy was a girl rumored to be close to Guy.
With the situation being what it is, I wonder why you seem suspicious.
Is the police's eyesight that poor? There are definitely more suspicious people out there.
There's something that's been bothering me.
What is it?
Well, actually, I heard that Guy and Kristy were married. Is that true?
Yes, they were buried together. It seems that was part of their will as well.
That's strange. Guy had decided not to marry Kristy.
I don't know the reason, but he definitely said at that party that he wouldn't marry Kristy.
Is that true?
It's not unreasonable to be suspicious. But I did hear that from him. So why...
What the truth was, I can no longer know.
Isn't he traveling with Guy?
Yes, that's right. But you heard about her going to see Ginny before, right?
I only know that she went to see Ginny. I don't know what kind of conversation happened or what took place there.
Thinking about it, she is indeed very suspicious. Just when I thought she traveled with Guy, Ginny's body was discovered.
But that doesn't make her the killer.
There’s an alibi, right? No, they are creating their own alibi to prove they're not the culprits.
Depending on how you look at it, anyone could seem suspicious.
I can't believe she ended up dead.
She didn't take her own life out of regret for her past sins, did she?
There are theories suggesting suicide.
Huh.
Hmm, that's an odd story.
Maybe she was happy to die alongside Guy.
Do you still think Jennifer is the culprit behind the serial murder case?
No, that was just a form of harassment. Right now, I can't even guess who the real culprit is.
But, among the people at Director Fabar's home party, including you, there might be the perpetrator of this series of murders.
So you think so too.
It's hard not to think so given the situation.
I'm relieved to not be involved any further.
I thought Kristy was suspicious. But now that she’s dead,
I thought the same, even as a friend. Perhaps Guy was involved too.
Well, if no more incidents happen now, maybe they'll...
You’re having a tough time, huh? Just when you think you’ve become a suspect, next you’re doubting your best friend and their lover.
Already...
I don't really think he’s the culprit, but I dislike the way he seems to enjoy my misfortune.
Surprisingly,
he seems to be a man with sharp insight and rich imagination. The director role might suit him well.
Maybe he’s not as bad as I thought.
Ginny, Eva, Cindy.
While there's some connection, there's no reason for me to have to kill them.
Now, it’s hard to even remember the alive Ginny. Why did the culprit target her?
Indeed, Ginny was murdered. Her wrist must have been cut, and she died slowly from excessive bleeding.
I wonder why he decided to marry Kristy, who once tried to break off the engagement.
Traveling is fine, but the fact that he hasn't told anyone the destination is troubling.
If Guy's death is a suicide, what could have been the reason? Was he afraid of being imprisoned as a serial killer?
It's only natural to think that way.
But I really don't want to believe that Guy committed murder.
To prove Guy's innocence, I have to find the real culprit myself.
Why did he leave without even telling me where he was going? Was he ordered to keep quiet?
Kristy would probably listen to anything Guy says. Did she not disclose the destination because he commanded her?
Even though they were able to reunite as lovers and even got married.
I never expected things would turn out like this.
Poor Kristy.
The fact that I'm a suspect is a bad joke. There are other people who should be looked at more closely. The police can't be relied on.
In any case, I must prove my own innocence.
If they say Guy committed suicide, then it's because he realized Kristy was the culprit and it was a double suicide to save her.
But I want to believe that neither Guy nor Kristy is the culprit.
I want to believe they weren't the ones responsible.
Surely, the incident isn't over yet. If I can atone for suspecting Guy and Kristy by finding the real culprit.
Excuse me for barging in.
Ah.
Right, I'm going to flee the country now. I might not be back for a while, so this could be the last time I see you.
Fleeing the country, huh? A tour overseas?
You're a man of few reactions. I'm going to spend three months in the United Kingdom. So today is our last meeting.
Take care.
What would you do if, by any chance, I was the culprit?
I can't imagine you killing Ginny. Let alone the thought of cutting off a hand.
Yeah, that's right. Well, I gotta get going soon. Do your best, but don't overdo it.
Thank you. You take care, too.
Thanks.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
